content: Hey, I just got back from an amazing sabbatical in Eastern Europe. I spent 3 months traveling and learning so much about the culture and history.
That sounds incredible! What made you decide to take a sabbatical, and what was your favorite part of the trip?

I've been working as a Civil Rights Attorney for 5 years, and I felt burned out. I needed a break to recharge and refocus. I visited 7 countries, including Poland, Hungary, and Croatia. I even got to try beekeeping in a small village in Bulgaria!
Beekeeping is such a unique hobby! Did you enjoy it? And how do you think your experience as a Civil Rights Attorney will be affected by your time off?

I loved beekeeping! I've actually been interested in it for a while, and I even took a course on it before my trip. As for work, I'm excited to get back to my job at the law firm in New York City. I've been thinking about taking on more cases related to environmental justice.
That's great to hear! Environmental justice is such an important topic. Have you considered learning any new skills related to that field?
Actually, I've been thinking about learning more about policy-making and advocacy. I've heard that the University of California, Berkeley has a great program for that.
Berkeley is a great school! I can definitely help you look into their program. What specific aspects of policy-making and advocacy are you interested in learning more about?

I'd like to learn more about how to effectively communicate with lawmakers and create policy change. I've been inspired by the work of my colleague, Rachel Chen, who's been working on a bill to protect national parks.

That sounds like a great goal! Effective communication is key in policy-making. I can provide you with some resources on that topic. Have you considered attending any conferences or workshops related to environmental justice?

Yes, I've been looking into attending the Environmental Justice Conference in Chicago next month. I've also been thinking about starting a podcast to discuss environmental issues with experts in the field.
A podcast is a great idea! I can help you brainstorm some topics and guests. What's your target audience for the podcast?
I'd like to target young adults who are interested in environmental issues but don't know where to start. I've been inspired by the work of Greta Thunberg and her ability to mobilize young people.
That's a great target audience! I can help you come up with some engaging topics and guests. Have you considered partnering with any organizations to promote your podcast?
I've been in touch with the Sierra Club, and they're interested in partnering with me. I'm excited to get started and make a positive impact!
That's great to hear! I'm excited to see where your podcast takes you. Let me know if you need any more help or guidance.
